summary.headline: Greater Kuching is in watch mode — the system is holding, but the weather-air mix can turn within an hour. 8.1mm expected in the next 6 hours — that's enough to overwhelm Sg. Maong if drains aren't clear.
summary.detail: Heat index 36.1°C · AQI 42 (APIMS ground) · 8.1mm rain/6h · 6 aircraft · 0/6 hydro stations · 57 headlines across 3 languages

hydroStations.name: Batu Kitang
hydroStations.note: Primary upstream gauge for Kuching urban flood pre-warning.
hydroStations.humanBrief: Upstream canary for all of urban Kuching. Kampung houses along this stretch sit ~1.2m above the historic flood line. When this gauge reads Alert, water is at doorstep level for ~340 riverside homes. The TPPA Batu Kitang landfill sits 800m downstream — a Danger reading risks contaminated runoff into the water treatment intake. 3 primary schools and 1 community health clinic within the 2km flood buffer.
hydroStations.lastEvent: Sg. Sarawak exceeded Warning (10.5m) on 2024-01-15; Jalan Batu Kitang flooded for ~4 hours.

hydroStations.name: Sg. Sarawak @ Buntal
hydroStations.note: Tidal indicator for Petra Jaya / north-bank flood risk.
hydroStations.humanBrief: Tidal gauge at the Sarawak River mouth. When high tide coincides with upstream rain, Petra Jaya's north-bank government district floods — the DUN (State Assembly) complex, federal offices, and the Civic Centre are all in the inundation zone. Worst case: king tide + upstream Warning creates a pincer effect that traps water in the urban reach for 8–12 hours.
hydroStations.affectedEstimate: Petra Jaya government district, ~15 federal/state offices
hydroStations.lastEvent: Tidal surge combined with upstream rain on 2023-11-28; water entered the Civic Centre car park.

hydroStations.name: Siniawan
hydroStations.humanBrief: Heritage town at the confluence of Sg. Sarawak Kanan tributaries. The weekend night market draws 2,000+ visitors on Fri/Sat evenings — a sudden rise during market hours creates a crowd-safety incident, not just a flood event. The old shophouses have no raised foundations. Padawan Fire & Rescue Station 2 is 4km upstream; response time is 12–18 minutes in dry conditions, longer when the access road floods.
hydroStations.affectedEstimate: ~800 residents, heritage shophouses, weekend market (~2,000 visitors)
hydroStations.lastEvent: Moderate flooding in 2024-03; market evacuated, 12 shophouses affected.

hydroStations.name: Kpg. Git
hydroStations.humanBrief: Remote upper-catchment gauge deep in Padawan's hinterland. Predominantly Bidayuh kampungs connected by single-lane roads that become impassable at Alert level. This gauge is the earliest signal of a basin-wide event — a rise here arrives at Batu Kitang 4–6 hours later. Mobile coverage is intermittent; JPS manual readings may lag by 2+ hours.
hydroStations.affectedEstimate: ~600 residents in scattered kampungs, limited road access
hydroStations.lastEvent: Gauge exceeded Alert during monsoon surge 2024-01-14; road to Kpg. Git cut for 2 days.

hydroStations.name: Sg. Maong
hydroStations.note: MBKS urban drainage canary — backs up first.
hydroStations.humanBrief: This is the urban drain that backs up first. Sg. Maong runs through MBKS's densest commercial corridor — MJC Batu Kawa, Stutong commercial area, and the BDC industrial zone. At Alert level, the Jalan Song/Stutong junction floods and traffic gridlocks across southern Kuching. At Warning, floodwater enters ground-floor retail. The drain's capacity has not been upgraded since 2012 despite 23% residential growth in the catchment.
hydroStations.affectedEstimate: ~12,000 residents, 200+ commercial properties, Jalan Song corridor
hydroStations.lastEvent: Flash flood on 2024-09-03; Stutong junction submerged for 3 hours, 40+ vehicles stranded.

hydroStations.name: Sg. Bedup
hydroStations.humanBrief: Boundary gauge between Padawan and Serian. The Sg. Sadong basin drains into agricultural lowlands — oil palm estates and pepper gardens. Flooding here disrupts the Kuching–Serian road (the only trunk route) and isolates kampungs for days. The Tebedu border crossing with Kalimantan is 30km upstream; cross-border water management is a diplomatic, not just engineering, issue.
hydroStations.lastEvent: Sg. Sadong exceeded Normal after sustained rain in 2024-02; road passable but waterlogged for 18 hours.

apims.summary: 1/2 ground stations reporting. Worst: Shanghai (上海) AQI 42 (Good).

operations.title: Pre-emptive drain clearance: Monitoring
operations.detail: MET Malaysia indicates Monitoring. Projected rainfall 8.1mm. Focus on Penrissen and Batu Kawa sectors via Padawan GCAP protocols.
operations.humanContext: Sg. Maong backs up first — Stutong/Jalan Song junction floods at 2.0m. ~12,000 residents and 200+ shops affected. Last flash flood (Sep 2024): 40 vehicles stranded, 3-hour gridlock.

operations.title: Urban growth pressure: 2,907,500 (Sarawak)
operations.detail: DOSM data shows continued rural-to-urban migration. Padawan housing stock needs 1675 unit buffer for 2026.
operations.humanContext: Padawan absorbed 68% of Greater Kuching's new housing since 2018 but only 22% of infrastructure spending. Drainage, schools, and clinics are lagging behind rooftops.

operations.title: Haze drift advisory prep
operations.detail: AQI 78. Coordinate with DOE stations for transboundary haze trajectory analysis.
operations.humanContext: Transboundary haze from Kalimantan fires follows prevailing SW winds Jun–Oct. Kuching's two APIMS stations (Kuching, Samarahan) are 40km apart — a reading at one doesn't guarantee the same at the other.

operations.title: Sarawak Data Sync: 1 sets
operations.detail: Latest update: Facts and Figures 2016. Land use compliance audit pending for Ward G.

operations.title: Green City Action Plan (GCAP)
operations.detail: Verify reforestation progress near Padawan wetlands using Sentinel-2 NDVI telemetry.
operations.humanContext: Padawan's GCAP targets 15% green cover increase by 2030. Current rate: ~2.1% since 2021. Wetland encroachment near Bako is the blind spot — satellite NDVI is the only way to monitor at scale.
